steps.title: Recognize the key finding
steps.content: The notes state “They found that certain fibers allow the brain to toggle from one method of processing smells to another” and then specify both methods: capturing information at a given moment, and tracking changes over time. An effective summary must present this discovery—the toggle mechanism and both processing approaches.

steps.title: Match the finding to the choices
steps.content: Choice D directly reports both methods of smell processing and the toggle between them—exactly what the study found. Choices A, B, and C fail to summarize the findings: A foregrounds the methodology instead, B describes only one method, and C describes the research goal rather than what was discovered. That matches choice D — D is correct.
